explanation: The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV mode (PCVMode), then the breathing cycle must be defined by the specified parameters. This translates to the inspiratory pressure being set to the target for PCV mode (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP), the breathing cycle time being determined by the respiratory rate (breathingCycleTime_equalTo_1_divideBy_RR_PCV), and the expiratory time being determined by the I:E ratio (ExpiratoryTime_equalTo_breathingCycleTime_divideBy_1_plus_ItoE_PCV). All these conditions must hold true when in PCV mode.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) mode, the breathing cycle is defined by the inspiratory pressure (Pinsp_PCV), respiratory rate (RR_PCV), and the inspiratory-to-expiratory time ratio (I:E PCV). This is captured by asserting that when 'PCVMode' is true, then simultaneously, the inspiratory pressure is set to its target value for PCV mode ('P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP'), the breathing cycle time is set according to the respiratory rate ('breathingCycleTime_equalTo_1_divideBy_RR_PCV'), and the expiratory time is set according to the I:E ratio ('ExpiratoryTime_equalTo_breathingCycleTime_divideBy_1_plus_ItoE_PCV'). This ensures that the defining parameters are active and correctly configured throughout the PCV mode operation.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode (PCVMode), then it must always be the case that the inspiratory pressure is set to the target inspiratory pressure for PCV (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP), the breathing cycle time is equal to 1 divided by the respiratory rate for PCV (breathingCycleTime_equalTo_1_divideBy_RR_PCV), and the expiratory time is defined by the breathing cycle time and the I:E ratio for PCV (ExpiratoryTime_equalTo_breathingCycleTime_divideBy_1_plus_ItoE_PCV). This captures the requirement that these parameters define the breathing cycle whenever the system is operating in PCV mode.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) mode, the breathing cycle is characterized by the specified parameters. 'PCVMode' serves as the condition (bool_exp1). The 'definition' of the breathing cycle by inspiratory pressure (approximated by 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP' as the closest available AP for inspiratory pressure in PCV context, despite the 'apnea backup' nuance), respiratory rate ('breathingCycleTime_equalTo_1_divideBy_RR_PCV'), and the inspiratory-to-expiratory ratio ('ExpiratoryTime_equalTo_breathingCycleTime_divideBy_1_plus_ItoE_PCV') are combined with a logical AND to form 'bool_exp3'. The LTL template 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)' ensures that these conditions are continuously met as long as the system is in PCV mode.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in PCV mode, the breathing cycle time must be defined by the respiratory rate (RR_PCV) and the expiratory time must be defined by the breathing cycle time and the inspiratory-to-expiratory ratio (I:E PCV). This captures the continuous nature of the requirement that these parameters define the breathing cycle while in PCV mode.

The property states that globally (G), whenever the system is in PCV mode (bool_exp1), it must always be the case that the breathing cycle is defined by the given parameters (bool_exp3). Bool_exp3 combines the two specific conditions mentioned for respiratory rate and inspiratory/expiratory ratio. This captures the 'shall be defined by' aspect as a continuous implication.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode (PCVMode), then the breathing cycle is defined by the inspiratory pressure (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP), the respiratory rate (breathingCycleTime_equalTo_1_divideBy_RR_PCV), and the inspiratory-to-expiratory ratio (ExpiratoryTime_equalTo_breathingCycleTime_divideBy_1_plus_ItoE_PCV). This captures the requirement that these parameters define the breathing cycle whenever the system is in PCV mode.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode (PCVMode), then the breathing cycle is defined by the inspiratory pressure being set (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP), the breathing cycle time being determined by the respiratory rate (breathingCycleTime_equalTo_1_divideBy_RR_PCV), and the expiratory time being set according to the I:E ratio (ExpiratoryTime_equalTo_breathingCycleTime_divideBy_1_plus_ItoE_PCV). This directly translates the 'shall be defined by' aspect into a persistent implication in PCV mode.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV mode (bool_exp1), then it is always true that the breathing cycle time is equal to 1/RR_PCV AND the expiratory time is equal to (1/RR_PCV) / (1 + I:E_PCV) (bool_exp3). This captures the definition of the breathing cycle based on respiratory rate and I:E ratio in PCV mode. The part of the prompt referring to "inspiratory pressure Pinsp_PCV relative to atmosphere" could not be translated using the provided atomic propositions, as no suitable boolean proposition exists to represent this specific definitional aspect of inspiratory pressure within the given list. The chosen template indicates an invariant property: whenever the system is in PCV mode, these definitional conditions hold.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in Pressure Controlled Ventilation mode (PCVMode), then the breathing cycle time is equal to 1 divided by the respiratory rate (RR_PCV) AND the expiratory time is equal to the breathing cycle time divided by 1 plus the inspiratory-to-expiratory ratio (I:E PCV). This captures the descriptive requirement that the breathing cycle parameters are defined by specific relationships when in PCV mode.
